title: ICC prosecutor targets DOJ, OSG
sub_topics: partial surrender of sovereignty via treaty
Article 5 of Rome Statute (genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, aggression)
Article 7 of Rome Statute (crimes against humanity)
OSG denial of state policy of killings

stances.claim: Sovereignty is not absolute — it is partly surrendered whenever the country enters into treaties; the Senate's ratification of the Rome Statute on August 30, 2011 bound the Philippines to ICC jurisdiction over crimes committed during membership.
stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal qualification of the absolutist sovereignty argument used by Duterte's defenders

stances.claim: Whether the drug-war killings were 'crimes against humanity' or 'ordinary crimes' is a factual matter that requires proof — at this point it deserves no comment beyond noting that the ICC prosecutor needs Pre-Trial Chamber authority to investigate.

Complementarity is the pivotal issue: the ICC will decline the case only if the DOJ and OSG can show (1) willingness and (2) ability to (3) genuinely investigate and prosecute the killings of the drug suspects.
stances.rhetorical_move: triadic doctrinal test stated as operative standard

stances.claim: If the Pre-Trial Chamber authorizes Khan and issues arrest warrants against Duterte and his officials, the country's reputation would be 'mercilessly debased and numbered among failed African states which have been ICC's prowling grounds.'
stances.rhetorical_move: appeal to national reputation + comparative shaming

stances.claim: The Philippine Supreme Court is admired as the most powerful in the world because it is the only tribunal constitutionally mandated to strike down grave abuse of discretion — its reputation should not be smeared along with the executive branch's.
stances.rhetorical_move: institutional defense of the judiciary + appeal to international recognition

events: Khan's request to resume preliminary investigation (2023)

signature_phrases: as a retired jurist, let me comment within my limited space
mercilessly debased and numbered among failed African states which have been ICC's prowling grounds
the most powerful in the world
register_markers: ALL CAPS opening clauses as section markers
Suffice it to say for now
decision_framework_signals: applies treaty-based partial-surrender-of-sovereignty doctrine to refute absolutist sovereignty defense
applies complementarity triadic test (willing + able + genuinely) as operative standard for ICC deferral
separates judiciary's institutional standing from executive's conduct to protect the Supreme Court's international reputation
notable_anecdotes: Personal friendships with US Justices Sandra O'Connor and Anthony Kennedy + visits with foreign chief justices (McLachlin, Xiao, Lebedev, Beinisch, Canivet) during his Supreme Court tenure
